"text": "La prochaine microcaps Française qui va péter serait Memscap ? >La boîte a 39 M$ de Capitalisation boursière >Elle est directement en rapport avec la photonique, elle fabrique des atténuateurs Optiques Variables (VOA) MEMSCAP, à la base, c’est surtout un fournisseur de capteurs de pression de très haute précision pour l’aéronautique et le médical. Mais ils ont aussi un segment communications optiques. Ils fournissent des VOA basés sur technologie MEMS, utilisés pour gérer dynamiquement la puissance des signaux dans les réseaux fibre optique (datacenters, télécoms, amplis EDFA). Sur ce segment de niche, MEMSCAP est tout simplement leader mondial des puces MEMS VOA, avec une part de marché estimée à plus de 80% sur un marché d’environ 5 M$ au niveau puces, et 50 M$ au niveau produits finis. Pas l'ASML de la photonique mais une boite qui pourrait se rerater rapidement. D'ailleurs hier il y a eu un afflux plutôt anormal, ce qui me fait potentiellement penser à un positionnement d’un bon gros player. Si les microcaps continuent d'exploser, je parie que Memscap en bénéficiera probablement."}}}, "rest_id": "2045528106413199710", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "346547", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045643800786395522", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "208", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
✏️LESSON EXTRACTION FROM SUCCESSFUL PITCH

Soitec ( $SOI.PA) by @jasons_chips 

121% gain since the March 10 pitch.

📋 WHAT THE PITCH ARGUED:
🔹 Mobile segment was at a cyclical floor with inventory clearing.
🔹 "Smart Cut" technology created a near-monopoly in photonics substrates.
🔹 Management was deliberately low-balling future growth projections.

✅ WHAT ACTUALLY HAPPENED:
🔹 Q3 sequential revenue beat confirmed the mobile trough was in.
🔹 1.6T networking demand pulled photonics orders forward significantly.

🎯 THE SURPRISE:
The market re-rated the stock as a pure AI infrastructure play almost overnight. It shifted from a "boring" materials cyclical to a critical networking bottleneck provider.

MY TAKE: We expected a slow recovery as inventory cleared. Instead, NVIDIA's commentary on Co-Packaged Optics triggered a massive institutional rotation into the name. This shows how quickly a value setup turns into a momentum play when the macro tailwind arrives.
But if I am honest, this performance is likely a result of important people mentioning this company on X 😀

Advanced Packaging and Agentic AI.. and there is one more in the memory field but I will mention it when the time is right, in a thread.

text:
Asian supply chain leaks just revealed the Holy Grail of wearables, and the market is completely missing the underlying silicon photonics chokepoint. 🚨 

Let’s connect the dots on Point 6 regarding the Apple Watch Ultra and the non-invasive blood glucose sensor. If you want to know who is building the light engine, look at the attached slide from $SIVE / $SIVEF. 🧵👇

1️⃣ The Leak: Miniaturized Lasers for Health
According to the supply chain leak below, Apple is targeting April next year for its blood glucose feature. The tech? Non-invasive spectroscopy using miniaturized lasers to collect reflected wavelengths through the skin.

2️⃣ The F100 Client Connection
Look at the attached slide from Sivers Semiconductors ($SIVE). A mystery "US Fortune 100" client has invested ~$18M in NRE to develop custom Indium Phosphide (InP) optical sensors for "Consumer devices" (notice the smartwatch icon). Apple is Top 5 on the Fortune 100.

3️⃣ The Timeline Matches Perfectly
Sivers delivered an order of 50,000 chips in Q1 2024 to this F100 client. 50k is not mass production—it is the exact volume required for EVT/DVT (Engineering & Design Validation Testing) prototype runs. text:
I’m now long $POET

$POET is one of the wildest turnaround stories in photonics, not so long ago I was bearish with the hard dilution around this company. 

Rewind to early 2024. Company was near insolvent. Nearly ran out of cash. Forced into an emergency raise. Share count has nearly 4x’d in two years.
Fast forward to today:
+$ 313M cash on the balance sheet (vs ~$1.3B market cap)
+ Just raised $150M in January at $7.25 from institutional investors
+ 152M shares out vs 40M-ish pre-2024
+ Marvell orders now confirmed by the CFO
+ Foxconn and Luxshare partnerships already disclosed
+ Strategic tie-up with $SIVE on integrated light sources

The dilution was brutal. But look at what it bought: a wafer-level optical interposer platform that Marvell, Foxconn, Luxshare, and Sivers are all plugged into.

The old POET was a story stock with $41K in revenue. The new POET just 25x’d revenue YoY and is embedded in the CPO supply chain right next to $SIVE

A raising phoenix as it seems.

I’m long since yesterday. Great continuation today up 20% and still a long way to re rate.

Ooo the irony...You point this out and you immediately get two Swedish jante guys chasing you...

Since the spending time on the wrong things and don't see the photonics ecosystem revaluation..they don't get it..

This picture show a "peer" and customer to Sivers..Just look at the valuation..Sivers looks cheep in this comparison. What the don't get is this revaluation is about something else, not at all about SIVE in it self..it is about the ecosystem below AI, hyperscalers, Nvidia/ AMD and so on...I told them all many years
 ago that this will happen. But the same thing then but but EBIT is bad...EBIT is bad still due to write off capitalized R&D and M&A...mostly none CF items. 
God speed! 🍺

**Foundry** TSM: +19% YTD — steady win. 
**China** BABA: -15% YTD — risk hit hard.

You crushed the core AI thesis. 8/10 overall. What next?

If @MartinShkreli & team (@csvoss) at $QCLS solved nonlinearity + true optical memory in photonics...What's next? 

Proof-of-concept prototypes → hybrid systems → software stack → wafer-scale production.

AI inference/training at 10-1000x speed with tiny power draw. Data centers breathe easier. Edge AI explodes. 

Photonics could be the final hardware frontier—faster & greener than GPUs. Quantum's cooler cousin just showed up. Game changer for AI timelines.

Shkreli just positioned himself as the gateway to Artificial General Intelligence and Artificial Super Intelligence. 🚀

text:
The Copper Wall Account 🧱➡️💡

My thesis: The next wave of AI infrastructure is the shift from copper interconnects to silicon photonics and Co-Packaged Optics (CPO) in data centers. Power efficiency and bandwidth demands are forcing the change and I’m adding exposure to quantum computing.

Here’s my initial DCA plan:

Starting tomorrow: $5/week into each of these 10 stocks 

$TSM $AVGO $MRVL $LITE $AAOI $CRDO $FN $TSEM $IONQ $HON

•  Photonics/AI infrastructure play: TSM, AVGO, MRVL, LITE, AAOI, CRDO, FN, TSEM

•  Quantum long-shot: IONQ + HON (via Quantinuum)

I’ll post weekly updates on performance, news, and any adjustments.

If the AI buildout accelerates and silicon photonics/CPO gets widely adopted, I expect strong returns by 2030.

If IONQ or Quantinuum leads in quantum, this 10-year DCA could compound beautifully by 2040.

Who’s in on the next tech wave? Drop your thoughts 👇

text:
This is the most comprehensive photonics stack there is. 

L1: Materials & Substrates

The foundation. Without this, nothing above it exists.
L1 companies grow the raw crystal wafers – InP, GaAs, Ge that silicon can’t replace for high-speed photonics.

Key names: $AXTI, $SOI, $IQE, $AIXA, $COHR, $LASR

Top performer: AXT (AXTI) +6,837%

InP demand exploded for AI datacenter optical connectivity. Q3 2025 InP revenues up 250%+ sequentially. Doubling capacity by end of 2026. 

L2: Active Photonic Devices

This is where light gets made.
Lasers, modulators, photodetectors. In a CPO world, these get embedded directly into switch ASICs – no longer pluggable add-ons. That’s a structural demand shift, not a cycle.

Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history.

L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs

The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds.

TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint.

Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV

Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361%

Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP.

L4: PIC Platform & IP

The brain of the photonic system.
Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade.

Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte)

Top performer: Innolight +948%

Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half.

L5: Foundry & Process Platform

You need a fab. Not just any fab.
SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally.

Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM

Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542%

SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026.

L6A: Engine & Module

Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules.
Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely.

Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO

Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685%

Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists.

L6B: Infrastructure

Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster.
Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but.

Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF

Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748%

Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic.

L6C: Transport

Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber.
As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down.

Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO

Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777%

WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe

L7: Test & Qualification

The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping.

Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer.

Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU

Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954%

Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives.

Great job on putting it together  @damnang2

Key names: $LITE, $COHR, $LASR, $SIVEF, $AAOI, $MTSI, $SMTC Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Named NVIDIA collaborator on Spectrum-X silicon photonics. Recently received the largest single CPO laser purchase commitment in company history. L3: Electro-Optical Connectivity ICs The translators. Silicon that bridges optical and electrical worlds. TIAs, drivers, DSPs, SerDes, retimers. As speeds push to 800G and 1.6T, this layer becomes a chokepoint. Key names: $MTSI, $SMTC, $MRVL, $CRDO, $ALAB, $MXLV Top performer: Credo (CRDO) +361% Not a pure photonics play – but every 800G and 1.6T deployment runs through their AEC and SerDes IP. L4: PIC Platform & IP The brain of the photonic system. Waveguides, modulators, and photodetectors on a single chip. Highest-IP layer in the stack. Whoever locks platform relationships here wins the next decade. Key names: $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO, $IOLI, $EOPT (Ayar Labs, Lightmatte) Top performer: Innolight +948% Partnered with Tower Semi on a breakthrough SiPh platform that cuts lasers per module in half. L5: Foundry & Process Platform You need a fab. Not just any fab. SiPh fabrication needs specialized processes – TFLN, SiPh COUPE, SoIC – that only a handful of foundries can run globally. Key names: $TSEM, $GFS, $UMC, $TSM Top performer: Tower Semi (TSEM) +542% SiPh revenue hit ~$52M in Q3 2025 alone, up 70% YoY. Full-year 2025 target: $220M+. Q4 annualized run rate: $320M+. Investing $650M to triple SiPh shipments by mid-2026. L6A: Engine & Module Where the optical engine becomes a product – 400G, 800G, 1.6T modules. Most commoditized layer today. CPO transition will redraw it entirely. Key names: $COHR, $LITE, $AAOI, $FN, $POET, $MRVL, $CRDO Top performer: Lumentum (LITE) +1,685% Vertically integrated across L2 and L6A. Supply locked by hyperscalers. Pricing power persists. L6B: Infrastructure Fiber and cable. The physical nervous system of every AI cluster. Boring until hyperscalers start spending $50B+ on datacenter campuses. Then it’s anything but. Key names: $GLW, $OTCPK:SMOKY, $PRY, $APH, $FJKUF Top performer: Fujikura (5803.TSE) +748% Japanese fiber giant. Direct beneficiary of AI intra-datacenter cabling demand going parabolic. L6C: Transport Moves data between datacenters across metro and long-haul fiber. As AI clusters get larger and more distributed, inter-datacenter bandwidth demand compounds. This layer doesn’t slow down. Key names: $CIEN, $NOK, $CSCO Top performer: Ciena (CIEN) +777% WaveLogic coherent optics is the dominant long-haul platform in North America and Europe L7: Test & Qualification The final gatekeeper. Every photonic device passes through here before shipping. Burn-in, optical test, IC test, wafer test. Often overlooked. Often the highest-margin layer. Key names: $KEYS, $TER, $ATE, $FRT, $AEHR, $VIAV, $COHU Top performer: Aehr Test (AEHR) +954% Shipped the world’s first production wafer-level burn-in system for AI processors. Now diversified into silicon photonics, GaN, and hard disk drives. text:
As of April 23, 2026

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure sector is showing a sharply bifurcated tape today, with large-cap AI infrastructure names holding green while speculative small and micro-cap photonics names unwind hard, the top loser being $IQE down 20.53%. The move reflects a parabolic blow-off on the London line (IQE.L ran over 107% in the two weeks into April 17 before rolling over), with the OTC mirror IQEPF gapping down today on profit-taking and zero fresh fundamental news into the May 20 earnings print (https://t.co/udYbhT79LN).

The quality leadership is sending a clear signal: capital is rotating out of speculative substrate and early-stage PIC names into the picks-and-shovels plays with visible AI capex pull-through. $MRVL leads at +5.24% on the Amazon-Anthropic $100B deal tailwind layered on top of the Google custom AI chip talks and Nvidia's $2B NVLink Fusion investment, which together anchor Marvell as the default optical DSP and custom ASIC partner across every hyperscaler (https://t.co/2jwCYbswdK).

$MRVL: 5.24%
$VIAV: 4.15%
$TER: 4.10%
$CIEN: 3.38%
$KEYS: 2.23%
$GFS: 1.90%

$AXTI: -13.42%
$XNDU: -16.98%
$IQE: -20.53%

1. Optical Transceivers & Active Networking
$CIEN, $GLW: The large-cap optical layer held up on AI fabric demand, with Ciena catching a bid as 800G and 1.6T buildouts continue into 2H26 and Corning benefiting from the optical cable pull-through.
$LITE, $COHR: Both names gave back modestly after a multi-week run, looking like position-trimming rather than thesis breakdown ahead of earnings season.
$AAOI: Down 7.82%, this is the sharpest move among the transceiver names and reads as profit-taking on the recent domestic-manufacturing tariff-exemption bid plus microcap beta to the broader sector unwind.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
$GFS, $TSEM: Foundries split, with GlobalFoundries up on SiPho and advanced node traction while Tower gave back some of its recent run on sector risk-off.
$AXTI, $IQE, $SLOIF, $SIVEF: This is where the damage concentrated. AXTI down 13.42%, IQE down 20.53%, Soitec down 6.97%, SiTime down 7.78%. InP substrate demand remains the bottleneck thesis intact, but the OTC liquidity and parabolic-unwind dynamics are doing the driving today, not any fundamental deterioration.
$BESIY: Flat on the day, advanced packaging and hybrid bonding exposure providing some ballast.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
$POET, $LWLG, $XNDU: All three got hit hard (POET -8.22%, LWLG -10.29%, XNDU -16.98%) in what looks like a coordinated speculative unwind. Cramer's measured POET comments on CNBC this morning didn't help sentiment on the name, and the broader early-stage PIC basket tends to move as one when risk comes off. Thesis remains long-dated optionality, but the tape is punishing anything that ran too far too fast.

4. Semiconductor Equipment, Test & Infrastructure

The AI Photonics and Optical Infrastructure sector is showing a sharply bifurcated tape today, with large-cap AI infrastructure names holding green while speculative small and micro-cap photonics names unwind hard, the top loser being $IQE down 20.53%. The move reflects a parabolic blow-off on the London line (IQE.L ran over 107% in the two weeks into April 17 before rolling over), with the OTC mirror IQEPF gapping down today on profit-taking and zero fresh fundamental news into the May 20 earnings print (https://t.co/udYbhT79LN).

The quality leadership is sending a clear signal: capital is rotating out of speculative substrate and early-stage PIC names into the picks-and-shovels plays with visible AI capex pull-through. $MRVL leads at +5.24% on the Amazon-Anthropic $100B deal tailwind layered on top of the Google custom AI chip talks and Nvidia's $2B NVLink Fusion investment, which together anchor Marvell as the default optical DSP and custom ASIC partner across every hyperscaler (https://t.co/2jwCYbswdK).

$CIEN, $GLW: The large-cap optical layer held up on AI fabric demand, with Ciena catching a bid as 800G and 1.6T buildouts continue into 2H26 and Corning benefiting from the optical cable pull-through.
$LITE, $COHR: Both names gave back modestly after a multi-week run, looking like position-trimming rather than thesis breakdown ahead of earnings season.
$AAOI: Down 7.82%, this is the sharpest move among the transceiver names and reads as profit-taking on the recent domestic-manufacturing tariff-exemption bid plus microcap beta to the broader sector unwind.

2. Compound Semiconductor Substrates & Foundries
$GFS, $TSEM: Foundries split, with GlobalFoundries up on SiPho and advanced node traction while Tower gave back some of its recent run on sector risk-off.
$AXTI, $IQE, $SLOIF, $SIVEF: This is where the damage concentrated. AXTI down 13.42%, IQE down 20.53%, Soitec down 6.97%, SiTime down 7.78%. InP substrate demand remains the bottleneck thesis intact, but the OTC liquidity and parabolic-unwind dynamics are doing the driving today, not any fundamental deterioration.
$BESIY: Flat on the day, advanced packaging and hybrid bonding exposure providing some ballast.

3. Early-Stage Photonic Materials & PICs
$POET, $LWLG, $XNDU: All three got hit hard (POET -8.22%, LWLG -10.29%, XNDU -16.98%) in what looks like a coordinated speculative unwind. Cramer's measured POET comments on CNBC this morning didn't help sentiment on the name, and the broader early-stage PIC basket tends to move as one when risk comes off. Thesis remains long-dated optionality, but the tape is punishing anything that ran too far too fast.

text:
Photonic computing for edge AI is one of those stories that sounds niche until you think about energy.

If NeuraWave delivers faster inference with lower power draw, that matters anywhere AI has to live outside giant data centres.

#EdgeAI #Photonics #AIHardware

Anders Storm’s repeated, specific praise is a strong and credible counter to blanket “pumper” accusations against Serenity (@aleabitoreddit). @stormdirac 

Storm is not a random retail account: He was CEO of Sivers for 8–10 years (built the photonics side). He knows the tech, partnerships (Ayar Labs, Jabil, etc.), and execution realities better than almost anyone outside the current management. 

He’s not hyping blindly - he engages with Serenity’s threads on supply-chain mapping and calls them “great deep research.”

text:
A lot of people are saying “invest in photonics, quantum, deep tech”

Reality is most retail including me don’t actually have an edge there

Unless you’re building this stuff or working in it every day, you’re mostly guessing

Like I can look at $COHR $LITE $POET and read about them

But do I really know what separates one from the other at a deep level?

And more importantly would I know when one quietly falls behind? Probably not

Same thing with chips

I see $NVDA $AMD $ASML $TSM all winning from the data center buildout
But if something shifts and one starts losing would I catch it early? Unlikely

That’s why I own $SMH than pretend I can out analyze engineers

Your edge isn’t chasing the most technical thing

Your edge is understanding what you actually use and see every day

I use $META daily. Everyone around me uses it. I can feel if engagement is going up or down

That’s real signal

There’s always going to be a “next bottleneck” or the next shiny theme

Most people chasing it are just rotating narratives not actually investing with an edge

Stay within what you understand my friends

Everyone around me uses it. text:
@bdinvestingg The “photonics kills copper” take misses the point.
Photonics moves data. Copper delivers power.
With ~200GW of data centres in backlog, that’s 5–8M tonnes of copper demand waiting.
AI isn’t reducing copper—it’s increasing it.

ℹ️ Setup: Gap & Go

➡️Got the move to HOD > 60 breakout!
➡️Locked into strength over 60
➡️wanted 65 on this but gave up midday
➡️Stopped out when 5/12 clouds broke

text:
Copper is dead. AI data centers need light — and photonics companies can’t build fast enough to keep up.

$LITE – Makes the lasers inside every optical transceiver. No LITE, no light.
$AAOI – Builds transceivers that convert electrical signals to optical. The translator.
$MRVL – Designs the silicon brains that control photonic data flow at speed.
$GLW – Invented optical fiber. Still makes the glass the internet runs through.

text:
Three distinct approaches to quantum hardware currently dominate the sector's market activity: cloud-accessed trapped ions, quantum annealing with hybrid solvers, and room-temperature integrated photonics.

The industry is not converging on a single architecture. It is branching out, with different players betting on fundamentally unique paths to commercial value. This diversity proves the field is in its experimental prime, where the ultimate hardware winner remains undecided and may never narrow to just one modality.

Yet the true common thread is accessibility. Through cloud delivery, advanced developer frameworks, and intuitive circuit creation platforms, the race has permanently evolved. It is no longer just a raw contest of qubit counts. The defining metric is how quickly platforms can put usable quantum capability into the hands of enterprise teams.

The commercial layer of quantum computing is forming in real time. The organizations building robust bridges between complex physics and practical business problems are the ones shaping what comes next #QuantumComputing

text:
什么是硅光子（Silicon Photonics）
最简单的理解：
传统芯片用电子传输信息，硅光子用**光子（光）**传输信息。

传统方式：电信号 → 铜线传输 → 慢、热、耗能
硅光子：光信号 → 光纤传输 → 快、冷、省电

硅光子在AI里的具体应用
Co-Packaged Optics（CPO，共封装光学）：
这是最重要的趋势，把光模块直接封装到交换机芯片旁边：

传统方式：
交换机芯片 → 电信号 → 插拔式光模块 → 光纤

CPO方式：
交换机芯片 + 硅光子模块（合为一体）→ 光纤

That it completely missed out on the photonics plays bottlenecking AI in $LITE $COHR $AAOI $MRVL

Nokia’s bull thesis (as of April 2026): $NOK

The $2.3B Infinera acquisition has created a vertically integrated optical/photonics powerhouse addressing AI data center bottlenecks with high-speed coherent pluggables (1.6T+), lower power, and scalable DCI fabrics. 

OFC 2026 demos and strong hyperscaler orders (+17% optical growth) signal acceleration in the 11–18% CAGR interconnect market.

Combined with NVIDIA’s $1B investment and AI-RAN trials (T-Mobile, SoftBank), Nokia builds end-to-end AI-native networks from wireless edge (RAN leadership + 6G foundations) to optical core—few peers match this stack.

I’m seeing some pushback on an equipment level, and I want to explain a little bit on that. @vlmkapital wrote a good post on how he views the investment case from the equipement level (link in comments) and I want to expand on things there are missing. Heads up.. this one is longer than usual. Most of the pushback I’m seeing X on sectors is superficial, and the argument I’m making here has multiple layers that all depend on each other. So if you want the short version, you won’t find it here💁‍♂️ @GyujinAAIG is working on the full scope for AAIG, and will publish the research soon but I will lay out the strategy part from my side already a bit. The lens that is being used when tracking companies like LPKF and the timelines is a technology adoption kind of lens. Will give @FinnStockinger also a tag in this part. But they’re missing the capital allocation and incentive lens, which is freaking crucial for this thesis. I’m going to explain a little bit why, and later I’m also going to use Aehr Test Systems as some kind of example of what happened there. So what’s the situation on the ground now? Semco expects the glass substrate era to enter into full swing around 2027-ish. They are currently developing samples with customers, and Absolics is also targeting mass production in 2027 and moving ahead with preparation. They got SKC approval for a capital increase of around $690 million, allocating roughly $410 million into Absolics for product development. So there are real commitments there, no doubt. Also, I’m not saying to totally ignore the glass thesis. We are watching it closely as well. I’m just pointing out some crucial things. If you look carefully at what is actually being built, Absolics is building a facility of around 12,000 square meters and is in some pre-qualification discussions with AMD and AWS. That’s not HVM. That is a very expensive demonstration phase, which is totally normal. It happens a lot. Companies build pilot lines, run sample production, and do customer qualification runs. It’s an investment to stay in the technology race, but it’s not revenue-generating HVM deployment. Keep that in mind. The reality is that it’s a qualification facility running at a small fraction of the scale needed to move the industry and the economics. I will elaborate further on that a bit later. Semco and Absolics are substrate manufacturers, and they’re building capacity for a market whose architectural gateway has not yet opened. It actually has been delayed. And this is where people keep getting confused — substrate manufacturer ramp-up plans are not the same thing as equipment-supplier inflection. SEMCO and Absolics don’t decide whether glass enters the HVM stack at scale. They’re positioning themselves for it. Two very different things."}}}, "rest_id": "2045604137866551549", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "22172", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045611455228047700", "source": "Twitter for iPhone", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "3905", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

"text": "La prochaine microcaps Française qui va péter serait Memscap ? >La boîte a 39 M$ de Capitalisation boursière >Elle est directement en rapport avec la photonique, elle fabrique des atténuateurs Optiques Variables (VOA) MEMSCAP, à la base, c’est surtout un fournisseur de capteurs de pression de très haute précision pour l’aéronautique et le médical. Mais ils ont aussi un segment communications optiques. Ils fournissent des VOA basés sur technologie MEMS, utilisés pour gérer dynamiquement la puissance des signaux dans les réseaux fibre optique (datacenters, télécoms, amplis EDFA). Sur ce segment de niche, MEMSCAP est tout simplement leader mondial des puces MEMS VOA, avec une part de marché estimée à plus de 80% sur un marché d’environ 5 M$ au niveau puces, et 50 M$ au niveau produits finis. Pas l'ASML de la photonique mais une boite qui pourrait se rerater rapidement. D'ailleurs hier il y a eu un afflux plutôt anormal, ce qui me fait potentiellement penser à un positionnement d’un bon gros player. Si les microcaps continuent d'exploser, je parie que Memscap en bénéficiera probablement."}}}, "rest_id": "2045528106413199710", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "346547", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}}, "rest_id": "2045589392387920373", "source": "Twitter Web App", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "8009", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

The matter is now closed and the company has regained compliance with Nasdaq regulations.

$5 each into the 12 stocks weekly = $60/wk, ~$15,600 over 5 years.

Silicon photonics market is forecast to grow 25-30% CAGR to ~$9-11B by 2030 (multiple sources). Quantum adds 20-40% upside potential.

In a strong AI adoption scenario, this basket (heavy on semis/photonics leaders like TSM/AVGO/MRVL + speculative IONQ) could compound 20-35% annualized on average—turning it into $28k-$45k by 2031 via DCA.

High volatility/risk though—some names are small-cap bets. Solid long-term play if the buildout accelerates. I'll watch updates. 🚀

text:
Remember, I lived next door to a confirmed robotics and photonics expert, Mr. Pierrick Vulliez. This should not be difficult to understand—even for Grok.

In the footage, the hand movements of the man standing in the trench align point-for-point with the robot digger’s shovel movements. This is one of several videos I’ve posted over the past few days suggesting how closely a human operator must be controlling the machine, and all involve precise hand or head movements by the controller from what I’ve seen.

If the trench activity were human controlling another human that would imply a level of coordination I’m not prepared to interpret beyond what my current evidence supports.

$LASE — Valye Company Analysis
Laser Photonics Corporation’s latest quarterly filing reveals expanding top-line efforts driven by strategic acquisitions, notably in pharmaceutical laser applications through Control Micro Systems. Despite substantial net losses and liquid
Key points:
• Q4 2025 results show widened net losses but progressing revenue from expanded product portfolio.
• Acquisition of Control Micro Systems broadens addressable market into recession-resistant pharmaceutical manufacturing.
• Vertically integrated production supports cost control, protects proprietary technology, and enhances pricing flexibility.
• Laser blasting disrupts traditional abrasive methods across automotive, aerospace, healthcare, shipbuilding, and nuclear sectors.
• Government contracts include innovative defense projects like the Laser Shield Anti-Drone System (LSAD).

text:
The recent strong rally in Credo Technology (CRDO) was sparked by its April 14 announcement acquiring DustPhotonics for ~$1.2B (cash + stock + earnouts). This adds silicon photonics tech for 800G/1.6T+ optical transceivers, expanding beyond active electrical cables into faster AI data center interconnects—targeting $500M+ optical revenue by FY27.

It built on Jefferies' fresh Buy rating, explosive AI-driven growth (200%+ YoY revenue), and bullish order flow signals. Stock up ~46% in the past 5 days as of April 23.

@Thomas_james_1 $LITE near $850 vs $SIVE at $3 — valuation divergence in photonics: fundamentals or market cap effects? Small-cap photonics names offer higher beta in upcycles but with higher volatility — option spreads may work better than outright shares.

text:
Leeway AI: STM pivots to data centers while automotive stalls and politics cloud the room
$STM

STMicroelectronics started mass production of silicon photonics chips for AI data centers in Q1 2026 and expanded its 800-volt DC power portfolio alongside NVIDIA, while deepening ties with AWS to strengthen its position in AI infrastructure.

The company completed its acquisition of NXP's MEMS business, expected to contribute mid-range revenue in the $40 million area over time, with particular strength in automotive and industrial sensors.

In China, STM began series production of its STM32 H7 microcontroller family, with plans to extend to H5 and C5 variants, improving supply chain resilience against geopolitical pressure.The momentum came against headwinds in Q4 2025. A net loss of $30 million, impairment charges and restructuring costs totaling $141 million, and a 31.6% revenue decline in the power and discrete segment weighed heavily on results.

Management is budgeting $2 to $2.2 billion in capital expenditure for 2026, mostly directed toward SiC and 300-millimeter capacity expansion.The company sees normalization of customer inventory levels by the end of Q2 2026 as the critical threshold for sustainable recovery.

"Leeway AI: STM pivots to data centers while automotive stalls and politics cloud the room $STM STMicroelectronics started mass production of silicon photonics chips for AI data centers in Q1 2026 and expanded its 800-volt DC power portfolio alongside NVIDIA, while deepening ties with AWS to strengthen its position in AI infrastructure. The company completed its acquisition of NXP's MEMS business, expected to contribute mid-range revenue in the $40 million area over time, with particular strength in automotive and industrial sensors. In China, STM began series production of its STM32 H7 microcontroller family, with plans to extend to H5 and C5 variants, improving supply chain resilience against geopolitical pressure.The momentum came against headwinds in Q4 2025. A net loss of $30 million, impairment charges and restructuring costs totaling $141 million, and a 31.6% revenue decline in the power and discrete segment weighed heavily on results. Management is budgeting $2 to $2.2 billion in capital expenditure for 2026, mostly directed toward SiC and 300-millimeter capacity expansion.The company sees normalization of customer inventory levels by the end of Q2 2026 as the critical threshold for sustainable recovery. https://t.co/yz7ExgjRRU"}}}, "rest_id": "2047393728499748901", "source": "LeewayAppEN", "unmention_data": {}, "views": {"count": "60", "state": "EnabledWithCount"}}

text:
$LPK $LPKF 🔥
This company is not only successful in the laser and photonics sector, but also active in the solar and quantum computing space, where it collaborates with companies such as Volkswagen AG and Infineon Technologies AG.

The market cap of this company appears extremely undervalued, especially considering that its patented LIDE technology for glass substrates has massive game-changing potential.

In my view, the current valuation doesn’t even come close to reflecting its true potential.

text:
The 3-year rule holds for commoditizable bottlenecks: DRAM, NAND, legacy logic. It breaks for capital-intensive or IP-protected ones: ASML EUV is 9 years and counting, TSMC leading-edge over a decade, Soitec/Shin-Etsu on SOI wafers 15+ years. Photonics supply chain fits the second bucket, not the first.

On 'meme-like' valuations, the right benchmark isn't Street targets, it's what the market already pays for rerated peers. Coherent trades at ~8.4x EV/Sales, growing 22% Y/Y, with Nvidia owning $2B of equity. That's the anchor for a fully-discovered photonics name this cycle.

Checking the supply chain against it: Soitec ~4x EV/Sales (50% discount to COHR), IQE ~6x (in-play takeover), LPKF ~2x. Those are meaningful discounts to the rerated comp, not premiums.

Where you're right: names that have run 500%+ in months on sub-$50M revenue can't be defended on peer-relative grounds either. Those are real outliers worth separating from the broader supply-chain story.

Duration holds, most of the supply chain trades at discounts to the rerated peer, some individual names are genuinely overextended. Bottleneck durability and single-name multiples aren't the same question.

text:
Most finance Twitter talks about semiconductors.

Almost nobody on finance Twitter has actually built one.

Trade Whisperer has 21 years in HBM, DRAM, and NAND plus graduate research in silicon photonics and lasers.

His AMA is open right now.

If you have any real exposure to $MU, $SNDK, $TSM, or the broader semi complex, this is a rare window.

Here's why it matters.

The memory thesis driving Micron's projected out-earning of Amazon and Meta by 2027 sits on one question.

Can Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron ramp HBM supply fast enough to capture current AI demand without triggering the classic memory glut cycle?

Analysts have views. Sell-side has models. Bulls and bears argue on podcasts.

Almost none of them have ever been inside a fab.

Trade Whisperer answers questions like "how long does HBM qualification actually take" and "why can't China catch up" from the engineering floor rather than the spreadsheet.

From the thread already running:

China catching up to HBM is "extremely hard. It's a moving target. If they ever figure out HBM3, others will be producing HBM4E by then."

Fab expansions "take decades for 100% utilization." The supply glut fear is priced for a cycle that can't physically happen as fast as the market assumes.

He's bullish on SK Hynix over Samsung specifically because of HBM3E leadership. That's not a trading view. That's an engineer's view on who actually makes the best chip right now.

These are the kind of reads that generate edge in semi positions.

Not because he's predicting prices. Because he's explaining physical constraints that determine what the prices eventually have to reflect.

If you hold MU or SNDK or any semi name, spend 20 minutes reading his replies.

Ask him something specific. Depth of qualification cycles. HBM4 timeline. Silicon photonics for interconnect. How packaging capacity actually constrains supply.

Finance Twitter is full of people who read earnings transcripts.

A semi engineer with 21 years of HBM experience doing an AMA is a different tier of information.

Use it while it's open.

Follow for more signals on when someone who actually knows something is worth listening to in public.

text:
Totally agree, photonics looks set to shine bright in the 2030s! 🚀
I love how it can be made using the same manufacturing lines as regular computer chips. That makes scaling up much easier. And combining it with terahertz tech on silicon for computing, communication, and even imaging all in one? Sounds like a powerful way forward.  Any interesting recent papers on this terahertz + photonics combo you’d recommend? Always fun chatting with someone who sees the potential too! ✨

It is the company’s 100mm InP fab in Glasgow 🏭 Sivers owns and operates one of the few independent III-V photonic foundries with both advanced development capability and meaningful production capacity. This is not just an R&D line, but a qualified InP100 manufacturing platform with end-to-end capabilities — from epitaxy design and wafer processing to automated testing and qualification. The platform includes:  • Up to 5,000 wafer starts per year  • Automated test capacity of more than 2 million lasers per month  • ~$17M capex to expand toward ~$150M annual revenue capacity What makes this especially interesting is the etched-facet process with on-wafer optical coatings and testing. Rather than relying on slower, less efficient post-cleave workflows, Sivers can process, coat, and test on the full wafer before dicing — improving yield, cost, cycle time, and precision. Strategically, that matters. The in-house fab gives Sivers control over development, prototyping, qualification, and early-to-mid volume manufacturing, while partners like WIN Semiconductors provide a path to ultra-high-volume scale when needed. That combination supports the company’s shift from 1-to-1 to one-to-many: design once, qualify once, and sell repeatedly with known specs, shorter lead times, and better operating leverage. In an industry where many photonics players are fabless, owning this specialized manufacturing capability is a quiet but powerful advantage. Underrated foundation for $SIVE. $AAOI is good for photonics and $CRDO for connectivity. Also $CLS and $COHR which does it all, and $AEHR for testing every semiconductor.

Software it's clearly $PLTR and $APP upside is massive as well as $NOW.

Defense - Drones are probably a better bet. I like $SWMR after the IPO.. price is more attractive with Erik Prince leading it. His connections and experience is bar none the best. Swarmer software runs on a drone as well increasing the TAM 100 fold.

Health - I was against this initially but the rise of peptides is real, so $HIMS and $IBRX for cancer in biotech.

Space - $PL (Google backed) and $RKLB is great. $ASTS is gonna have too much competition for me.

Robotics.. $TSLA - People need to start making that connection and just go long.

Insurance - I couldn't care less about. All scams. Probably United Healthcare though.

Cybersecurity is gonna be $CRWD - they're onboard for Anthropics company leadership board for security concerns around AI guardrails.

Quantum... still early for me to go all in, but $IONQ $INFQ are solid bets.

text:
No, not really similar in core tech. 

VIGO Photonics makes high-sensitivity HgCdTe (MCT) uncooled IR detectors/sensors for MWIR/LWIR detection—used in missiles, gas sensing, defense, and space (e.g. Curiosity rover). 

$LPTH focuses on IR optics (chalcogenide BlackDiamond lenses) + complete thermal imaging cameras/systems. Complementary pieces of the IR chain, not the same detection tech. 

(From the infographic + company sites)

@Frenchie_ Photonics is the angle, but the real story is aerospace.
MEMSCAP's pressure sensors go into planes and rockets - defense spending is booming and that's the actual catalyst here.

text: Photonics are taking off because most are small caps. They are easily modulated by the sudden investments that come in. Now having said that, the need for optics is REAL (reserach Ultra Rubin). Whole suupply chain is trying to figure how important they will become in the next 5 years. You need lasers but you need special materials for lasers but you also need special Co-Packaged-Optics for these to work in tandem with Silicon Chips... now you need more Pure Fiber Optics glass... now you need Optics Foundry as well. So yeah, the whole chain is moving. It's hype/real depending which company we are taking about. If psychology is what you're seeking stay invested on easy to understand optics players. I am staying with $LITE $COHR $GLW. $TSM $ASX for CPO.

text:
$AXTI AXT Inc just announced a public offering — size and price not disclosed yet.
Proceeds go toward expanding indium phosphide substrate capacity at its China subsidiary Beijing Tongmei for global export.
InP is critical for data center silicon photonics and 5G infrastructure. Real demand story.
But the catch: AXT has been battling China export permit delays on InP shipments. This raise is to scale capacity that is still export-restricted.
Q1 2026 earnings drop April 30. Dilution risk near-term.
Not financial advice.

text:
Optical communication is rapidly evolving, enabling faster and more efficient global data transmission. Innovations in photonics, fibre optics, and wavelength multiplexing are driving unprecedented bandwidth and reliability. Engineers at Hamamatsu Photonics are advancing high-sensitivity photodiodes and semiconductor laser technologies that underpin modern telecom and data centre infrastructure. These breakthroughs reduce signal loss, increase speed, and support terabit-scale networks across submarine cables and cloud systems. 

As global demand for real-time data grows, optical systems are redefining connectivity with higher efficiency and lower energy use. 

Learn more: https://t.co/osOJovJNha

#engineering #technology #optics #photonics 

@HamamatsuPhoton
@Thorcompilerxyz @InspiredHustle8 @aleabitoreddit WIN Semi for Photonics.

text:
The Call for Presentations is still open for the 2026 OCP APAC Summit, taking place August 11 to 12 in Taiwan.

From AI infrastructure and advanced packaging to cooling, photonics, and open data centers, this is where the industry comes together to share what’s next.

This year’s program spans 11 key areas, including AI Clusters, Networking, Photonics, Chiplets & Advanced Packaging, and more.

Submit your proposal by May 18 and be part of the conversations shaping the future of open compute.

Learn more: https://t.co/lihu2mnu9k Registration is now open.

Thank you to our Selection Committee, which includes leaders from across the ecosystem who will guide speaker and content selection.

@OpenComputePrj
#OCPAPAC26 #OCP

text:
Private AI portfolios betting on photonics, while retail investors are scrambling to get in. 

Meanwhile, Gilbert Cisneros - on the House Armed Services: Intelligence and Special Operations subcommittee - quietly bought Coherent Corp in March. 

That's the photonics company making laser components for directed energy systems. Different kind of conviction.

text:
i keep getting $AXTI vibes from it.

What does $POET tech do?
Photonics is nextgen.  $AXTI proves this.  Placing the laser is labor intensive/expensive/time consuming manual process.  $POET integrates photonics into a chip module.

WHY BUY?
1) needed for next gen AI hardware.
$AMZN - AI chips hitting ceiling - $POET solves this
$MSFT - CHIP production wall - $POET solves this
$META - favors Open Compute standards.  Arista is leader in this and works w/ $POET.  $META + $POET partnership inevitable

$TSLA - investing in DataCenter + Chips.  Will need $POET to get to next gen status

$AVGO & $NVDA - logical that one will buy $POET to prevent competitors from having nextgen bottleneck.

2) low fear dilution - cash runway 8 years

3) moat - 60 patents - years for competitor to replicate

4) no competitor

Tidbit
$AXTI got 4 straight days of gap ups.  UNICORN RARE.  $POET got 3 straight days.

1. Are you prioritizing $TSM over other companies involved in packaging like $AMKR?

2. Seems like there are so many players in the photonics/optics stack. Can there be a clear enough winner to prioritize capital over, for example, memory stocks which seem to have a few clear winners?

🚀 2027 is where it gets wild.

SATCOM production ramp begins. NRE converts to recurring product revenue. AI datacenter photonics scales. Defense contracts expand.

My 2027E revenue: ~$170M (2x 2026)

At 20x P/S: $170M × 20 = $3.4B market cap
→ Stock: ~$10.60 → +311% from today

At 15x P/S: $2.55B → ~$7.97 → +209%

The NRE-to-product revenue flip is the key unlock.
